content.text: Why Training and Attestation Are Enforcement Prerequisites

Technical controls can stop many AUP violations, but they cannot substitute for employee understanding. A proxy block that prevents access to an unapproved AI tool will generate a help-desk ticket, not compliance. When employees understand why the AUP exists — the specific harms it prevents and the personal consequences of violations — they make better judgements in the grey areas that no policy can fully enumerate.

Attestation — the formal act of an employee confirming they have read, understood, and agree to comply with the AUP — also serves a legal function. In disciplinary proceedings, the attestation record establishes that the employee was on notice of the policy before the violation occurred. Without it, a claim of ignorance has more weight. In regulatory examinations, it demonstrates that the organisation exercised reasonable care in communicating its controls to staff.
Training without attestation is an awareness programme. Attestation without training is a legal formality. Both together constitute a defensible compliance record.
Training and Attestation Lifecycle

attrs.alt: AUP training and attestation lifecycle: five phases from onboarding to event-triggered refresh

content.text: The lifecycle has five phases that together cover the full employment relationship:
Onboarding — core AUP training completed before or on first day of GenAI tool access.
Attestation — digital signature confirming comprehension and agreement; access gate does not open without it.
Access grant — IAM role provisioned after attestation record is confirmed in the LMS.
Annual refresh — delta module covering any AUP changes; re-attestation required; access suspended if overdue.
Event-triggered remediation — targeted module assigned after a violation or after a major AUP revision.
Core Training Module Design
Content Structure

The core module is the foundation all employees complete. It should take no longer than 60 minutes. Content must be scenario-driven: abstract policy statements are forgotten; concrete examples of permitted, conditional, and prohibited actions stick.
Section
Duration
Learning Objective
Why GenAI governance matters
8 min
Employee understands specific harms the AUP prevents
The three-lane model (permitted / conditional / prohibited)
15 min
Employee can classify a use case into the correct lane
Data classification and prompt hygiene
12 min
Employee knows what data must not appear in prompts
Conditional use request process
8 min
Employee knows how to obtain approval for a conditional use
Shadow AI and approved tools
7 min
Employee understands why unapproved tools are prohibited
Violations, consequences, and how to report
10 min
Employee knows the escalation path and personal consequences
Assessment Gate

The module ends with a 10-question assessment. A score of 80% or above is required to proceed to attestation. After two failed attempts, the employee's manager is notified and a live refresher session is scheduled with the L&D team.
Role-Based Training Tracks
General Staff Track

All employees with access to any approved GenAI tool complete the core 60-minute module. The annual refresh is a 20-minute delta module that covers changes since the last attestation — new tools added to the approved list, updated conditional-use requirements, or new prohibited categories.
Technical and Developer Track
Engineers, data scientists, and others who integrate GenAI via API complete the core module plus a 45-minute technical supplement. The supplement covers:
API authentication and secret management — never hard-code API keys; use vault-based secret injection.
Prompt injection risk — understanding how untrusted input can subvert system prompts in agentic workflows.
Output validation — why GenAI output used in code or data pipelines must be validated before downstream consumption.
Supply chain risk — evaluating open-source model weights and third-party fine-tuned models against the approved-tool requirement.

# Secure API key management — the AUP-compliant pattern
import boto3

def get_api_key() -> str:
    """Retrieve API key from secrets manager — never from literals or source code."""
    client = boto3.client("secretsmanager", region_name="eu-west-1")
    secret = client.get_secret_value(SecretId="prod/genai/openai-api-key")
    return secret["SecretString"]

# AUP-prohibited patterns (never do these):
# api_key = "sk-abc123..."       # Hard-coded credential
# os.environ["KEY"] = "sk-..."   # Env var set in source
High-Risk Function Tracks
Employees in HR, Legal, Finance, and customer-facing functions complete the core module plus a function-specific supplement:
Function
Supplement Focus
Duration
HR / People
Employment law basics; DPIA for AI-assisted HR decisions; protected-characteristic redaction
40 min
Legal / Compliance
AI output review obligations; not-legal-advice disclaimer requirements
35 min
Finance
Prohibited autonomous financial decisions; audit trail for AI-assisted analysis
30 min
Customer-Facing
Customer content review gate; hallucination risk; brand guidelines
30 min
Engineering / Platform
As Technical Track plus: deployment review gate for AI features in production
45 min
Digital Attestation Design
What the Attestation Must Capture

A legally defensible attestation record includes more than a checkbox. It must capture sufficient detail to prove that a specific employee agreed to a specific version of the AUP at a specific time on a specific device.

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class AttestationRecord:
    record_id: str
    employee_id: str
    employee_name: str
    employee_role: str
    aup_version: str           # e.g. "v2.3"
    aup_effective_date: str
    attestation_timestamp_utc: str
    ip_address: str            # source IP at time of signing
    device_id: Optional[str]   # endpoint management device ID if available
    training_record_id: str    # links to the passing TrainingRecord
    attestation_text_hash: str # SHA-256 of AUP text as presented to employee
    
    def display_text(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"I, {self.employee_name}, confirm that I have completed the GenAI "
            f"AUP training (completion ID: {self.training_record_id}) and agree to "
            f"comply with the GenAI Acceptable Use Policy version {self.aup_version}, "
            f"effective {self.aup_effective_date}. I understand that violations may "
            f"result in disciplinary action up to and including termination."
        )
    
    def is_current(self, current_aup_version: str) -> bool:
        return self.aup_version == current_aup_version
Record Retention Requirements
Attestation records must be retained for a minimum of three years from the date of signing, or one year after the employee's departure, whichever is longer. The retention system must:
Be separate from the LMS so that a single system failure cannot destroy both training completion and attestation evidence.
Produce records in a format exportable for legal discovery or regulatory inspection without specialist tooling.
Apply access controls so employees can view but not modify or delete their own records.
Produce a tamper-evident audit trail showing any administrative access.
Completion Tracking and Access Gating
LMS Integration with IAM
The training and attestation system integrates with IAM so that access to approved GenAI tools is automatically gated on compliance status:
New employee is provisioned in IAM but not yet in the 'genai-approved-users' group.
LMS assigns the core training module with a due date of 5 business days after start date.
Employee completes training, passes assessment, and signs attestation.
LMS webhook fires to IAM provisioning service with employee ID and attestation record ID.
IAM adds employee to 'genai-approved-users' group; tool access is enabled within 15 minutes.
For annual refresh: 30-day advance notification sent; access suspended if not complete by due date + 14 days.

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class TrainingCompletionEvent:
    employee_id: str
    module_id: str
    attestation_record_id: str
    aup_version: str
    completed_at: str
    is_refresh: bool   # True for annual refresh; False for initial onboarding

class IAMProvisioningService:
    def __init__(self, iam_client):
        self.iam = iam_client
    
    def handle_completion(self, event: TrainingCompletionEvent) -> None:
        action = "restore_group_membership" if event.is_refresh else "add_to_group"
        getattr(self.iam, action)(
            user_id=event.employee_id,
            group="genai-approved-users",
            reason=f"AUP training completed: attestation {event.attestation_record_id}",
        )
        self.iam.audit_log(
            action="genai_access_provisioned",
            user_id=event.employee_id,
            attestation_record_id=event.attestation_record_id,
            aup_version=event.aup_version,
            timestamp=event.completed_at,
        )
    
    def suspend_overdue(self, employee_id: str, due_date: str) -> None:
        self.iam.remove_from_group(
            user_id=employee_id,
            group="genai-approved-users",
            reason=f"Annual AUP refresh overdue since {due_date}",
        )
        self.iam.audit_log(
            action="genai_access_suspended_overdue",
            user_id=employee_id,
            due_date=due_date,
        )
Completion Rate Reporting
Training completion rates are a governance metric reported quarterly to the AI governance committee:
Overall completion rate: percentage of employees with current attestation, by department.
Overdue rate: percentage whose annual refresh is past due, by department and manager.
Time-to-complete for new joiners: distribution of days from start date to first attestation.
Assessment failure rate: percentage requiring more than one attempt — flagged for curriculum review if above 15%.
Event-Triggered Training
Post-Violation Remediation
When an AUP violation is confirmed, the corrective response includes mandatory re-training targeted at the specific violation type:
Violation Severity
Remediation Training
Re-Attestation Required
Timeline
LOW
15-min targeted micro-module on violated section
No
5 business days
MEDIUM
Full core module retake
Yes
3 business days
HIGH
Full core + function-specific module; live debrief
Yes
1 business day post reinstatement
CRITICAL
Full curriculum; CISO-led debrief; witnessed re-attestation
Yes (witnessed)
Scheduled as part of return-to-work plan
Major AUP Revision Triggered Training

When the AUP is revised in a way that changes permitted, conditional, or prohibited categories, a delta module is created. All employees with current attestation receive it within 30 days. The AUP version number is incremented; existing attestations are marked as attesting to the prior version until the delta is completed and the new attestation is signed.
Summary

An effective AUP training and attestation programme creates a documented audit trail that demonstrates employee awareness, closes the 'I didn't know' defence in disciplinary proceedings, and gates GenAI tool access on verified compliance.
All employees complete a 60-minute core module and pass an 80% assessment gate before attestation and access grant.
Technical and high-risk-function employees complete role-specific supplements covering API security, employment law, or domain obligations.
Digital attestation records must capture employee ID, AUP version, timestamp, device, and training record link — retained three years minimum.
LMS-to-IAM integration automates access gating: provisioned only after attestation; suspended 14 days after annual refresh due date.
Post-violation remediation training is mandatory and proportionate: LOW gets a micro-module, CRITICAL gets a full curriculum with witnessed re-attestation.
Completion rates are a quarterly governance metric — assessment failure rate above 15% triggers curriculum review.
